item.title: parseInt/parseFloat NaN propagation in CLI commands
item.intent: All numeric CLI options must reject non-numeric input with a clear error message. NaN must never propagate silently into cron schedules, interval timers, or config fields.
item.description: Multiple CLI option parsers use parseInt/parseFloat without guarding against NaN. Key cases: (1) src/cli/index.ts:1920 — digestHour: Math.max(0, Math.min(23, parseInt(opts.digestHour, 10))) — Math.max(0, NaN) = NaN, so a non-numeric --digest-hour writes NaN into the cron schedule; (2) src/cli/index.ts:1959 — memoryLifecycleInterval: Math.max(1, parseFloat(opts.memoryLifecycleInterval)) * 3600_000 — same issue; (3) src/cli/index.ts:2705 — maxEgressDomains: parseInt(opts.maxEgress, 10) — no NaN check, written directly to config; (4) src/cli/index.ts:1845 — lineCount: parseInt(opts.lines, 10) — no NaN guard before use. Pattern: the dashboard port on line 3183-3184 already validates correctly (isNaN check + clear error). Extend that pattern to the 4 affected options. Steps: (1) digestHour — validate isNaN before clamping, exit with error if NaN; (2) memoryLifecycleInterval — same; (3) maxEgressDomains — add isNaN guard; (4) lineCount — add isNaN guard. Scope: ~8 additions across one file. Pattern to follow: lines 3183-3184 in same file.
